title: rosa rubiginosa subvar. rotundifolia
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subvar. rotundifolia, also known as the fragrant dog rose or wild rose, is a subvariety of the fragrant rose (Rosa rubiginosa) native to Europe and western Asia. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties. Rosa rubiginosa subvar. rotundifolia has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and menstrual disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support overall health and well-being, and its benefits include reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality.
